mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present value estimates and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 210, it signals persistent inflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins and forces risk-off behavior, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the high inflation signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous collapse in housing starts and retail sales indicates severe demand destruction across the economy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime, an unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% confirms labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when the economy is in an expansionary regime, or fails to predict negative returns during confirmed recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects robust real economic activity and corporate output, generating positive sentiment and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the short term.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant payroll growth combined with rising unemployment indicates hidden labor market fragility and underemployment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns, or the signal fails to outperform a baseline model in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales fall below 360,000 million dollars, it signals a sharp contraction in consumer demand, forcing earnings downgrades and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the low sales signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate is cut, real rates remain restrictive, signaling policy ineffectiveness and stagflation risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over the 20-day horizon.
